differential_diagnoses: Rheumatoid arthritis presenting with systemic involvement
Paraneoplastic syndrome (e.g., paraneoplastic arthropathy and neuropathy)
Undiagnosed hypothyroidism with associated myopathy and neuropathy
next_steps: Order comprehensive rheumatologic panel: ESR, CRP, RF, anti-CCP, and ANA.
Evaluate for cause of B6 deficiency: assess for occult malabsorption or dietary inadequacy.
Perform diagnostic joint aspiration or imaging (ultrasound/MRI) of MCP/PIP joints to confirm inflammatory arthritis.
Order thyroid function tests (TSH, free T4) to ensure replacement adequacy.
Involve physical and occupational therapy for assessment of mobility and activities of daily living.
Re-evaluate patient for occult malignancy if inflammatory markers are significantly elevated and autoimmune workup is negative.
